name: rake
description: Rake is a Ruby build program and task runner similar to Make, widely used in Ruby and Ruby on Rails projects for database migrations, asset compilation, testing, and custom automation tasks.

subcommands.name: db:create
subcommands.description: Create the database from DATABASE_URL or config/database.yml for the current RAILS_ENV

subcommands.name: db:drop
subcommands.description: Drop the database for the current RAILS_ENV

options.name: DISABLE_DATABASE_ENVIRONMENT_CHECK
options.description: Set to 1 to allow dropping production database

subcommands.name: db:migrate
subcommands.description: Run all pending database migrations

options.name: VERSION
options.description: Migrate to a specific version timestamp

options.name: RAILS_ENV
options.description: Run migrations in a specific environment

options.name: VERBOSE
options.description: Set to false to suppress migration output

subcommands.name: db:rollback
subcommands.description: Rollback the last database migration or a specified number of steps

options.name: STEP
options.description: Number of migrations to roll back (default: 1)

subcommands.name: db:seed
subcommands.description: Load seed data from db/seeds.rb into the database

subcommands.name: db:schema:load
subcommands.description: Load the database schema from db/schema.rb, recreating tables

subcommands.name: db:schema:dump
subcommands.description: Dump the current database schema to db/schema.rb

subcommands.name: db:test:prepare
subcommands.description: Check for pending migrations and load the test schema from the development schema

subcommands.name: db:reset
subcommands.description: Drop, recreate, and reload the database from schema.rb and run seeds

subcommands.name: db:setup
subcommands.description: Create the database, load schema, and run seeds

subcommands.name: db:migrate:status
subcommands.description: Display the status (up/down) of each migration

subcommands.name: db:migrate:redo
subcommands.description: Rollback the last migration and re-run it

subcommands.name: assets:precompile
subcommands.description: Compile all the assets listed in config.assets.precompile

subcommands.name: assets:clean
subcommands.description: Remove old compiled assets from public/assets

subcommands.name: routes
subcommands.description: Print all defined routes in the application, including HTTP verbs and controller actions

subcommands.name: middleware
subcommands.description: Print the Rack middleware stack for the application

subcommands.name: about
subcommands.description: Display information about the application environment (Ruby, Rails, database, middleware versions)

subcommands.name: spec
subcommands.description: Run all RSpec specs (available when rspec-rails gem is installed)

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Run all Minitest tests in the test/ directory

subcommands.name: notes
subcommands.description: List all annotations (TODO, FIXME, OPTIMIZE) found in the codebase

subcommands.name: tmp:clear
subcommands.description: Clear session, cache, and socket files from tmp/

subcommands.name: log:clear
subcommands.description: Truncate all or specific log files in log/

subcommands.name: stats
subcommands.description: Report code statistics (LOC, classes, methods) for the application

subcommands.name: secret
subcommands.description: Generate a cryptographically secure secret key

subcommands.name: zeitwerk:check
subcommands.description: Check that all autoloadable constants are correctly defined

globalOptions.name: --tasks
globalOptions.description: Display tasks with descriptions, optionally filtering by a pattern

globalOptions.name: --all
globalOptions.description: Show all tasks including those without descriptions

globalOptions.name: --describe
globalOptions.description: Describe tasks in detail

globalOptions.name: --where
globalOptions.description: Show where each task is defined

globalOptions.name: --execute
globalOptions.description: Execute some Ruby code and exit

globalOptions.name: --trace
globalOptions.description: Turn on invocation/execution tracing and enable full backtrace

globalOptions.name: --verbose
globalOptions.description: Log message to standard output

globalOptions.name: --dry-run
globalOptions.description: Do a dry run without executing actions

globalOptions.name: --rules
globalOptions.description: Trace the rules resolution of Rake rules

globalOptions.name: --no-search
globalOptions.description: Do not search parent directories for the Rakefile

globalOptions.name: --prereqs
globalOptions.description: Display task prerequisites in the dependency chain

globalOptions.name: --quiet
globalOptions.description: Do not log messages to standard output

globalOptions.name: --rakefile
globalOptions.description: Use the specified Rakefile instead of searching

globalOptions.name: --rakelibdir
globalOptions.description: Auto-import any .rake files in the specified directory

globalOptions.name: --require
globalOptions.description: Require a Ruby library before executing Rakefile

globalOptions.name: --system
globalOptions.description: Use system-wide (global) rakefiles

globalOptions.name: --multitask
globalOptions.description: Treat all tasks as multitasks (run prerequisites in parallel)

globalOptions.name: --jobs
globalOptions.description: Number of parallel jobs (default: CPU count)

globalOptions.name: --version
globalOptions.description: Display the version of Rake

globalOptions.name: --help
globalOptions.description: Display help message

detectors.name: rakefile_exists
detectors.description: Detect Rakefile in the current directory

detectors.name: lib_tasks_dir
detectors.description: Detect lib/tasks directory for custom Rake tasks
